data.title: Micron in charts: Revenue from Computer and Networking grows most among units in FQ2
Perpetua plans official loan application to U.S. Export-Import Bank in Q2
data.description:
Perpetua Resources (NASDAQ:PPTA) said Thursday it plans to submit a formal loan application to the U.S. Export-Import Bank for its Stibnite gold and antimony project in Idaho in this year's Q2.

Alongside its full-year results, Perpetua (NASDAQ:PPTA) said the Export-Import Bank would determine if a final loan commitment might be issued.

The company had received a non-binding letter of interest from Ex-Im in April 2024 for a loan worth as much as $1.8B to develop the Stibnite project its antimony and gold mine, Reuters reported in April last year; if the loan is approved, the gold and antimony project would be one of the largesst U.S. investments ever in a mine.

The mine, which Perpetua (PPTA) hopes will open by 2028, could supply more than 35% of annual U.S. demand for antimony, a metal used to make solar panels, flame retardants and weapons, among other items; China accounted for 60% of globally mined antimony in 2024, according to data from the U.S. Geological Survey, and has banned exports of the metal to the U.S.

data.title: U.S. stocks end lower as investors gear up for $4.5T options test
data.description:
Wall Street on Thursday closed in the red, as traders expressed some caution a day ahead of a major quarterly options expiration event.

Some positive economic data on the housing market helped overcome a wobbly start, but the three major averages were unable to hold on to those gains. After some mixed trading, they turned decidedly lower.  

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) ended -0.22% at 5,662.87 points, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) settled -0.33% at 17,691.63 points. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) concluded -0.03% at 41,953.02 points.

Friday marks a "triple witching" day - an occasion on which stock options, stock index futures and stock index options are set to expire together. Citi estimates about $4.5T of contracts will expire.

Earlier, shortly after the opening bell, data from the National Association of Realtors showed total U.S. existing home sales rising 4.2% from January to 4.26M in February, easily beating the estimate of 3.95M.

The economic calendar also had the Conference Board's monthly leading indicator index update. Long seen as a recession indicator, the index declined further in February. 

"Today, the consensus among analysts and strategists seemed to be that the bottom of the correction is likely in. However, everyone also seems to agree that volatility is here to stay," Leo Nelissen, part of investing group iREIT+HOYA Capital, told Seeking Alpha.  

"While I’m not sure about the end of the rally, I like the risk/reward of value stocks, as retail investor sentiment has been horrible. Today, the AAII reported four consecutive weeks of more than 55% bearishness, something that has never happened in its history. If the Trump Administration can refrain from announcing new (unexpected) tariffs and if new data confirms a 'no landing' scenario, the market is likely to bounce back in the days ahead," he added. 

Market participants overnight tempered some of the euphoria sparked by Fed chair Jerome Powell calling tariff-induced inflation "transitory" on Wednesday.

A rally in bonds triggered by the Fed extended to Thursday, sending U.S. Treasury yields lower. The rally had lost some steam, with the 10-year yield (US10Y) up slightly to 4.24%, and the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) down 2 basis points to 3.97%. 

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.  

Turning to active stocks, Accenture (ACN) ended as the top percentage loser on the S&P 500 (SP500), -7.3%, after the IT and consulting services provider said Elon Musk's push for government efficiency had led to a negative impact on sales and revenue.

data.title: Nike gains after topping profit estimates, pointing to brand momentum
data.description:
Nike (NYSE:NKE) gIBW in postmarket trading on Thursday after delivering a better-than-feared FQ3 earnings report with new CEO Elliot Hill at the helm for a full quarter.

Revenue decreased 9.3% year-over-year on a reported basis to $11.3 billion, with declines seen across the North America, Europe Middle East & Africa, and Greater China regions. Total footwear revenue was down 12% to $7.2 billion vs. $7.1 billion, while apparel revenue slipped 2% to $3.2 billion vs. $3.0 billion consensus.

Nike Direct was down 12% from a year ago to $4.7 billion. Equipment revenue was down 2% to $477 million. Converse sales growth lagged during the quarter, with an 18% decline due to softness across all territories.

Gross margin for the Oregon-based company decreased 330 basis points from a year ago to 41.5% of sales, to miss the consensus mark of 41.9%. The margin decline was primarily due to higher discounts, higher inventory obsolescence reserves, higher product costs and changes in channel mix, partially offset by restructuring charges in the prior year.

Net income for the apparel giant was down 32% year-over-year to $0.8 billion. EPS slotted in at $0.54 vs. $0.28 consensus and $0.77 a year ago.

Nike (NYSE:NKE) ended the quarter with inventory down 2% from a year ago at $7.5 billion. Nike's (NYSE:NKE) cash position at the end of the quarter was $10.4 billion, vs. $10.6 billion a year ago. 

"Our outlook for the second half of fiscal 2025 driven by our 'Win Now' actions remains consistent with what we communicated last quarter," highlighted CFO Matthew Friend. "The operating environment is dynamic, but what matters most for NIKE is serving athletes with new product innovation and re-igniting brand momentum through sport," he added.

Shares of Nike (NKE) moved up 4.8% in postmarket trading to $74.90 after shedding 1.6% during the regular session. The negative revenue growth for the quarter was already baked into the stock.

Nike (NKE) typically guides during the earnings conference call. Companies that have a very high trading correlation with Nike following its earnings report include Under Armour (UAA), Foot Locker (FL), Skechers (SKX), Lululemon (LULU), Adidas (OTCQX:ADDYY), and Deckers Outdoor (DECK).

data.title: KINDERCARE LRN Non-GAAP EPS of $0.09 beats by $0.04, revenue of $646.96M in-line
data.description:
• KINDERCARE LRN press release (NYSE:KLC): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.09 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $646.96M (+4.7% Y/Y) in-line.
  • Based upon current estimates, we expect revenue for the full fiscal year 2025 to be approximately $2.75 billion to $2.85 billion, adjusted EBITDA to be approximately $310 million to $325 million, and adjusted net income per common share, diluted, to be approximately $0.75 to $0.85. The fiscal year 2025 outlook includes a 53rd week, which will contribute $45 million to $50 million of revenue and $10 million to $12 million of adjusted EBITDA.

data.title: Ouster GAAP EPS of -$0.48 beats by $0.11, revenue of $30M beats by $0.2M
data.description:
• Ouster press release (NASDAQ:OUST): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.48 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $30M (+22.7% Y/Y) beats by $0.2M.
    • GAAP gross margin of 44%, compared with 22% in the fourth quarter of 2023 and 38% in the third quarter of 2024.
    • Non-GAAP gross margin1 of 44%, compared with 35% in the fourth quarter of 2023 and 45% in the third quarter of 2024.
    • Net loss of $24 million, compared with $39 million in the fourth quarter of 2023 and $26 million in the third quarter of 2024.
    • Adjusted EBITDA1 loss of $10 million, compared with $14 million in the fourth quarter of 2023 and $10 million in the third quarter of 2024.

    Full Year 2024 Highlights

    • Revenue of $111 million, an increase of 33% compared with fiscal year 2023.
    • Shipped more than 17,300 sensors for revenue.
    • GAAP gross margin of 36%, compared with 10% in fiscal year 2023.
    • Non-GAAP gross margin1 of 41%, compared with 30% in fiscal year 2023.
    • Net loss of $97 million, compared with a loss of $374 million in fiscal year 2023.
    • Adjusted EBITDA1 loss of $42 million, compared with a loss of $84 million in fiscal year 2023.
    • Ended 2024 with cash, cash equivalents, restricted cash, and short-term investments balance of $175 million.
    • Fully repaid all outstanding balances under revolving credit line utilizing cash on hand.
    • Increased software-attached bookings by over 60% as compared with fiscal year 2023.
    • Expanded bookings for Ouster Gemini and BlueCity deployments to more than 700 sites.

data.title: Planet Labs GAAP EPS of -$0.12 misses by $0.05, revenue of $61.55M misses by $0.33M
data.description:
• Planet Labs press release (NYSE:PL): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.12 misses by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $61.55M (+4.5% Y/Y) misses by $0.33M.

  • For the first quarter of fiscal year 2026, ending April 30, 2025, Planet expects revenue to be in the range of approximately $61 million to $63 million. Non-GAAP Gross Margin is expected to be in the range of approximately 58% to 60%. Adjusted EBITDA loss is expected to be in the range of approximately ($3) to ($2) million for the quarter. Capital Expenditures are expected to be in the range of approximately $11 million and $16 million for the quarter.

  • For fiscal year 2026, ending January 31, 2026, Planet expects revenue to be in the range of approximately $260 million to $280 million. Non-GAAP Gross Margin is expected to be between approximately 55% to 57%. Adjusted EBITDA loss is expected to be between approximately ($13) million and ($7) million. Capital Expenditures are expected to be in the range of approximately $50 million and $65 million for the year.
